label: Narrow-shell 1BR / 1 bath, FRONT-BACK SPLIT — public front band (great room left with the entry, galley kitchen along the right wall) and private rear band (bedroom left, bath right). Kitchen and great room sit side-by-side with a dining counter (breakfast bar) dividing them; kitchen does NOT touch the rear wall — the bath stacks behind it on the plumbing line. Based on the narrow-fp-01 reference plan (4 x 6 m unit).

adjacencies.note: bedroom (rear-left) and great room (front-left) stack in the left column — shared wall only, NO door (2026-07-16 revision: the door moved to the kitchen seam so the great room's rear wall stays solid/furnishable). Bedroom keeps this public adjacency for the validator's bedroom-access rule.

bedroom's only door — opens west-to-east into the kitchen aisle at the rear end of the galley (2026-07-16 revision, per client direction: entering the bedroom from the kitchen side frees the great room's full wall run for furniture). 1.2 m floor = 0.9 m clear door + frames.

great room and galley kitchen sit SIDE-BY-SIDE in the front band (vertical seam) — open plan with a dining counter (breakfast bar) along the shared edge. counter_side: 'great' puts the band INSIDE the great room (kitchen keeps its full 1.5 m aisle), with the 2 stools beside it in the great room. min_shared_wall_m 2.1 = 2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass-through. Requires ldk_horizontal (the default kitchen/great must-stack rule would forbid this arrangement).
bedroom and bath share the rear band side-by-side — shared wall only, no door, per the locked no-bath-through-bedroom rule.

bath (rear-right) stacks directly behind the kitchen (front-right) on one plumbing line — shared wet wall AND the bath's only door, opening south into the kitchen aisle. Same guest-accessible-without-crossing-the-bedroom pattern as the squarish 1BR sibling.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_nw_front_back_split_bath_gr
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: Ultra-compact narrow unit (reference plan is 24 sqm total). Bath floors at 2.6 sqm (client soft minimum — the reference Banheiro is ~2.7) and caps at 4.5 so snap_gaps can't balloon it. Bedroom caps at 9.5 (reference Quarto is ~8.6) so the freed area flows to the great room instead of an oversized bedroom crowding the public band.

notes: Second 1BR entry in the narrow catalog, and the first FRONT-BACK SPLIT narrow
topology. Modeled on the narrow-fp-01 reference plan (a 4.00 x 6.00 m unit,
24 sqm): public front band with the entry into the great room (Sala de
Estar/Jantar) and a galley kitchen (Cozinha) along the right wall; private
rear band with the bedroom (Quarto, ~3.45 m deep) left and the bath
(Banheiro, ~1.80 m deep) right. Dining happens at a counter between kitchen
and great room (counter_divider with 2 stools), not at a table.
Layout intent (front at bottom):
Rear:  [BEDROOM        | BATH   ]
Front: [GREAT ROOM ][|||| KITCHEN]   (|||| = dining counter + stools)
[porch/entry]
Canonical fit: 8 x 10 m lot with standard 2 m setbacks -> exactly the
reference's 4 x 6 m envelope (shell ratio 0.67, 'deep' category).
Global-rule overrides this topology depends on (all topology-scoped,
zero effect on the rest of the catalog):
- kitchen_rear_pin: false — the bath stacks BEHIND the kitchen, so the
hardcoded kitchen-touches-rear-wall pin is impossible here. The
zone_split horizontal escape can't be used instead: the front/rear
seam is STEPPED (bedroom ~3.45 deep vs bath ~1.8; kitchen ~4.2 vs
great ~2.55), and zone_split needs one straight split line. This flag
was added for exactly this topology (2026-07-16).
- ldk_horizontal: true — kitchen and great room are side-by-side; the
default LDK must-stack rule and the kitchen-right-half symmetry break
both contradict that.
- private_area_floor: false — single bedroom (~8.6 sqm) can never
outweigh great+kitchen (~12+ sqm); same rationale as the squarish
1BR sibling.
- aspect_overrides kitchen 3.2 — the reference galley runs ~1.4 x 4.2
(3:1), past the catalog's 2.5:1 kitchen cap.
- kitchen mechanical_vent — the galley's exterior walls are the front
(entry side) and right; on tight envelopes the window may not reach
the 10% floor, and the open counter edge borrows the great room's
light anyway.
match_widths [bedroom,great] + [kitchen,bath] gives one straight vertical
seam between the left and right columns (the reference has a slightly
stepped seam — bath wider than kitchen — but the clean grid is within
~15 cm of the reference proportions and far more robust to solve).
No dirty kitchen declared — the kitchen fronts the street side, so there
is no sensible rear dirty-kitchen placement; briefs should also set
kitchen_back_door: false (the kitchen's rear wall is interior, shared
with the bath). service_area stays available in the rear setback behind
the bath (shares the plumbing line).
No fallback declared — the squarish 1BR sibling targets a different
shell category, so a shell-mismatch fallback would be misleading.
